findings.rationale: The Food Traceability List includes many specific food categories, while the GS1 class covers only fruits and vegetables, making our term a broader concept.
findings.qaRationale: FoodTraceabilityList is an enumeration of FDA regulatory food categories subject to FSMA 204 traceability, whereas gs1:FruitsVegetables is a product-attribute class scoping fruit/vegetable properties; they are different kinds of concept with no genuine subsumption.

findings.rationale: The candidate property refers to general traceability information, whereas our property specifies the particular FDA Food Traceability List category for a product.
findings.qaRationale: gs1:traceability is a link to track-and-trace information resources, whereas our property is a regulatory scope classification asserting which FDA Food Traceability List category a product belongs to — different concepts sharing only the word 'traceability', not a genus-species relation.
